answer:
The recurrence processes numbers by repeatedly halving (roughly), so the depth of recursion is about \(\log_2 N\). At each level, the added cost is \(\log N\), but note that the argument to the log shrinks as we go down. The total cost sums to about \(\log N + \log(N/2) + \log(N/4) + \dots\), which is \(O((\log N)^2)\).

\boxed{E}
